scenario.context: Station 5 of the daily run: apply technical and organizational measures and the security process.
scenario.mission: You derive suitable measures and place them into a traceable security process.

station.profile: Role-based access, approvals, security process, and follow-up

customerRequest.subject: Station 05: derive TOMs and security process for the work area
customerRequest.body: Please derive suitable technical and organizational measures for a sensitive work area and place them into a clear security process.

questions.title: Most effective immediate measure
questions.prompt: Which measure often provides the greatest immediate benefit for sensitive workstation data?

options.text: Assign access by role and review it regularly
options.rationale: This limits unnecessary access and reduces misuse risk.

options.text: Loosen approval rules to speed up processes

options.rationale: That is ineffective as a standalone measure.

feedback.correct: Correct: Role-based access is a central TOM building block.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: the measure must be effective and operationally viable.

questions.title: Identify technical and organizational measures
questions.prompt: Which points are sensible TOMs for the work area? (Multiple selections)

options.text: Role-based approvals and regular permission reviews

options.rationale: Organizational safeguards are part of it.

options.text: Current protection measures and governed updates
options.rationale: Technical baseline protection must remain stable.

options.text: Clear responsibilities for implementation and control
options.rationale: Only then does effectiveness remain traceable.

feedback.correct: Correct: You connect technology, organization, and follow-up sensibly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: connect access, process, and responsibility more strongly.

questions.title: Security process with measure planning
items: Implement measures and record responsible parties
Capture framework requirements and security policy
Check effectiveness and document residual items
correctOrder: Capture framework requirements and security policy
Implement measures and record responsible parties
Check effectiveness and document residual items

feedback.correct: Correct: The process moves cleanly from requirements to effectiveness checks.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: sequence framework, assessment, prioritization, implementation, and follow-up clearly.

questions.title: Calculate implementation status
questions.prompt: Out of 5 prioritized measures, 4 are already implemented. What is the implementation status in percent?

feedback.wrong: Divide implemented measures by the total and convert to percent.

questions.title: Formulate a measure proposal
questions.prompt: Write in 4-6 sentences a measure proposal with priority, responsibility, and a residual point.
questions.expectedOutline: Two strong measures, clear responsibility, and a transparent residual point.

feedback.correct: Correct: The proposal is effective and traceable.
feedback.wrong: Please make priority, responsibility, and residual point clearer.
